title: A Letter to Them Today
subtitle: A journal prompt set for writing to someone you have lost
excerpt: A journal prompt set structured as a letter to someone you have lost. The prompts move from where you are now through what you want to tell them and what you still need from them.
sourceNotes: Letter-writing to the deceased is a widely used grief-processing technique in bereavement counselling. The framing here is original to homemade.education.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you have things you want to say to the person you lost that you never got to say, or things that have happened since.
practice.whenNotToUse: This practice can be intense. If you are in acute grief or have not yet had support for your loss, consider using it alongside a counsellor or grief group rather than alone.

content.text: A journal prompt set structured as a letter. You are writing to the person you lost. The letter does not have to be sent anywhere. It is written for you.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
journal prompts as practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
What is happening in my life right now that they would want to know about?
Start with the news. What has happened since you last could have told them.
Where am I with my grief today?
Tell them. They are the right person to tell.
Closer in
What do I want to say to them today that I didn't get to say?
Write it. All of it. This is the version of the letter that doesn't have to be careful.
What do I still need from them?
Name it specifically. Not what you needed before the loss. What you need now, from the person they were.
What do I think they would say back?
Write their reply. You knew them. You know what they would say. Let yourself write it.
When you have finished writing, close the journal and take a few minutes before returning to the day.
Where this practice comes from

Letter-writing to the deceased is a widely used grief-processing approach in bereavement counselling, discussed in grief literature since the 1970s. The framing here is original to homemade.education.
